name: Long Beach
highlights: 16 km stretch of wild Pacific coastline, longest beach in Canada accessible by road

name: Wild Pacific Trail
highlights: 10 km trail hugging Ucluth Peninsula; Lighthouse Loop and Ancient Cedars section

name: Meares Island Big Tree Trail
highlights: Water-taxi to largest remaining intact temperate rainforest; Tla-o-qui-aht Tribal Park
bookingWindow: Water taxi from Tofino waterfront — no advance permit required

name: West Coast Trail
highlights: 75 km multi-day, most demanding trail on VI; permit required, open May-Sep
bookingWindow: Permit required — book at reservation.pc.gc.ca, opens in January

name: Juan de Fuca Marine Trail
highlights: Day hike sections including Mystic Beach and Botanical Beach; dramatic tide pools

name: Schooner Cove Trail
highlights: 2 km trail to secluded cove on Long Beach's north end; tide pools at low tide

name: Surfing — Cox Bay
highlights: The beginner break at Tofino — consistent, long, forgiving waves on a wide sandy bay
Cold water (10–14°C year-round) and real surf culture — this is not a resort amenity
Multiple surf schools including Pacific Surf School and Tofino Surf Adventures; 4/3mm wetsuit mandatory
September through November brings the biggest consistent swells for advancing surfers
bookingWindow: Book at pacificsurfschool.com — walk-in for rentals

name: Kayaking — Clayoquot Sound
location: Clayoquot Sound — launches from Tofino waterfront
highlights: 350,000 hectares of UNESCO Biosphere Reserve islands, inlets, and old-growth shores — wilderness within 20 minutes of Tofino
Grey whales, black bears, and Steller sea lions visible from the water
Remote Passages and Tofino Sea Kayaking Company are the established operators; independent rental for experienced paddlers
Multi-day expeditions possible with proper experience
bookingWindow: Book at remotepassages.com — guided half-day tours most accessible

name: Tofino to Long Beach — Bike Path
location: Tofino townsite → Cox Bay → Long Beach (Pacific Rim Highway)
highlights: Separated multi-use path connecting Tofino to Long Beach through the park — the most pleasant way to move between town and the beach without a car
Passes Cox Bay, Chesterman Beach, and enters Pacific Rim National Park Reserve
E-bikes and standard bikes available from multiple Tofino operators; flat and accessible for all fitness levels
Sunset ride back into Tofino along the inlet is one of the great low-key experiences on the coast
bookingWindow: Self-guided — bike rental from Tofino Bike Company or similar operators in town

name: Whale Watching — Clayoquot Sound
location: Tofino — multiple operators depart from the harbour
highlights: Gray whales migrate through Clayoquot Sound March-April and feed in the area through October. Humpbacks arrive May-September. Resident orcas year-round.
Zodiac tours get closer to the water; covered boats are warmer and drier. Both work — choose based on your tolerance for cold spray.
Remote Passages, Jamie's Whaling Station, and The Whale Centre are the established operators — all have marine biologist guides
Dress for Pacific weather regardless of the forecast. Layers, waterproof outer, warm hat. You will get cold.
bookingWindow: Book 2-3 days ahead in summer. Spring whale season (Mar-Apr) is less crowded.

name: Hot Springs Cove
location: Maquinna Marine Provincial Park — 37 km north of Tofino, accessible only by boat or floatplane
duration: full day (including 1.5 hr boat each way + 2 km boardwalk)
distance: 2 km boardwalk from dock to springs
highlights: Natural geothermal pools cascade down rock shelves directly into the Pacific — the hot-cold contrast at high tide is extraordinary
Accessible only by boat (1.5 hrs from Tofino) or floatplane (20 min). The journey through open Pacific and sheltered inlets is half the experience.
A 2-km old-growth boardwalk through Maquinna Marine Provincial Park connects the dock to the springs — Sitka spruce, nurse logs, silence
Arrive early on the first boat to have the pools before the midday tours arrive. The upper pools are hottest (50°C+); the lower pools mix with tidal Pacific.
bookingWindow: Book boat transport through Remote Passages or Tofino Water Taxi. Reserve 2+ days ahead in summer.

name: Mountain Biking — Cumberland
location: Cumberland, BC — 2.5 hrs from Tofino via Highway 4 and Highway 19
highlights: World-class singletrack in a former coal mining town — Cumberland is Vancouver Island's mountain biking capital with 200+ km of trail
Trails range from smooth flowy green runs to technical black diamond — the network is deep enough for a multi-day MTB trip
The town itself is a charming village with craft breweries, cafes, and a mountain culture vibe. Riders & Rogue is the local bike shop and community hub.
For corridor itineraries only — Cumberland is 2.5 hrs from Tofino. Worth the drive for serious riders.
bookingWindow: Self-guided — free trail access. Bike rental at Riders & Rogue in Cumberland.

name: Storm Watching — Pacific Winter Storms
location: Tofino and Ucluelet — any west-facing beach or headland
highlights: November through February, Pacific storms drive 20-foot swells directly into the Tofino/Ucluelet coast — the hotels were built for this
Wickaninnish Inn, Pacific Sands, and Long Beach Lodge all have storm-watching rooms with floor-to-ceiling windows facing the open Pacific
Walk the Wild Pacific Trail in Ucluelet during a storm for the full-body experience — rain gear, waterproof boots, humility before the ocean
This is not passive watching. The sound, the spray, the force of the water against the headlands is a physical experience. Dress for immersion.
bookingWindow: No booking needed for public beaches. Storm-watching hotel packages available Nov-Feb.
